rows.notes: Topmost line is a page heading. The first word reads clearly as 'Compulsus' (Latin form) but the remainder is ambiguous; I read 'suprascriply' though the handwriting could be 'supra scriply' or 'suprascriptly'. Final word is very unclear (I show '??amina'); letters look like 'amina' but initial letter is uncertain. No amounts present.

rows.description: - his [crossed out] el Approbatum per N?7
rows.notes: Second heading line contains heavily crossed-out text and a fragment that looks like 'el Approbatum per N?7'. The line is partially struck-through and several letters are indistinct; I preserved the visible structure ('Approbatum per') but the preceding word is mostly obliterated. No amounts.
Name line is clear: abbreviation 'Geo:' (George) followed by 'Paynter' and a trailing 'R' (possibly an initial or mark). Handwriting is legible and there are no monetary figures.
Clear reading of 'Jo:' (John or Joseph) and 'Cooke' with 'sub R' following. The colon and spacing match the original. No amounts visible.

First name abbreviation 'Geo:' is clear. Surname appears as 'Verman' in the ink but could plausibly be 'Vernon' or 'Vreman' depending on a single ambiguous letter (the middle vowel/consonant stroke). No amounts present.
Abbreviation 'Ric:' (Richard) and surname 'Hawkins' are legible; the downstrokes and loops match 'Hawkins' clearly. No monetary columns or figures on this line.

Given name abbreviation 'Dan:' is clear. Surname reads approximately as 'Alborn' though letters could be 'Alborne', 'Giborne' or similar; the trailing 'dec' (likely 'dec.' = deceased) is visible but the preceding name letters are moderately ambiguous. No amounts.
Abbreviation 'Hen:' (Henry) and surname 'Edwards' are clearly written and legible, including the final period. No monetary values present.
